title: Add ItacaLab - graphic editor for authors to image
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Books need covers too
gist.blurb: ItacaLab just sprouted in the community greenhouse. It's a graphic editor built specifically for authors to generate their own visuals, because judging a book by its cover is still very much a thing.
gist.summary: Added ItacaLab, a graphic editor tailored for authors, to the community apps showcase.
gist.impact: Writers can now discover ItacaLab in the greenhouse to generate and edit artwork for their literary projects.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a graphic designer for books. She should be wearing a tiny painter's beret and working at a retro computer with a stack of books nearby, blending the concepts of writing, graphic editing, and our ecosystem.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, using a retro computer monitor to design a book cover. A stack of colorful pixelated books sits next to the glowing lime green (#ecf874) screen. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, using a retro computer monitor to design a book cover. A stack of colorful pixelated books sits next to the glowing lime green (#ecf874) screen. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
